message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-core: cap CursorState::from_cbor decode at 64 KiB

CursorState::from_cbor previously called ciborium::de::from_reader on
whatever bytes the caller passed (the primary caller is
`cursor_state_from_object`, which feeds `ConnectorStateObject::state_cbor`
— a plain `Vec<u8>` bounded only by the outer object's 64 MiB CBOR cap).

CursorState is a tiny struct (i64 + optional String + u64); a legitimate
encoding is well under 256 bytes. A malicious or corrupted
ConnectorStateObject could embed a multi-megabyte `state_cbor` and force
ciborium to allocate / canonicalize it before the downstream non-canonical
re-encode check would reject it.

Fail closed at the entry point with `SerializationError::PayloadTooLarge`.
Cap = 64 KiB (MAX_CURSOR_STATE_BYTES) — two orders of magnitude above any
realistic cursor state.

Tests:
- cursor_state_from_cbor_rejects_oversized_payload: MAX+1 returns
  PayloadTooLarge with exact len/max fields.
- cursor_state_from_cbor_accepts_at_cap_boundary: exactly MAX bytes
  bypasses the size gate and fails on CBOR parse, proving the cap is
  inclusive (off-by-one safe).

Verified locally: cargo test -p fcp-core --lib cursor_state_from_cbor →
3 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
